skill.name: java-springboot
skill.description: Get best practices for developing applications with Spring Boot.
skill.summary: Expert guidance for building production-ready Spring Boot applications with modern Java practices.
skill.author: github

security_audit.summary: All 36 static findings are false positives. The external_commands detections are markdown backtick formatting for code references (e.g., pom.xml, @Service). The weak_crypto detections incorrectly flagged 'RESTful' and 'description' as cryptographic issues. This is a legitimate Spring Boot best practices document containing no security vulnerabilities or malicious code.

content.value_statement: Writing Spring Boot applications requires understanding of dependency injection, configuration management, and security patterns. This skill provides expert guidance for building maintainable, secure, and testable Spring Boot applications.
actual_capabilities: Provide guidance on project structure using Maven or Gradle with Spring Boot starters
Recommend dependency injection patterns using constructor injection with immutable fields
Advise on externalized configuration using application.yml and @ConfigurationProperties
Suggest Spring Security implementation with BCrypt password encoding
Guide development of RESTful APIs using DTOs, validation annotations, and @ControllerAdvice error handling
Recommend testing strategies using JUnit 5, Mockito, test slices, and Testcontainers
limitations: Does not generate complete application code or project templates
Does not provide debugging support for runtime errors or exceptions
Does not cover infrastructure topics like Docker, Kubernetes, or cloud deployment
Does not include database-specific optimization or query performance tuning

use_cases.title: New Spring Boot Project Setup
use_cases.description: Get guidance on organizing your project structure, choosing build tools, and configuring dependencies using Spring Boot starters for a maintainable foundation.
use_cases.target_user: Java developers starting a new Spring Boot application

use_cases.title: Building Secure REST APIs
use_cases.description: Learn how to design REST endpoints properly, use DTOs for data transfer, implement validation, and handle errors consistently across your API.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers building web services with Spring

use_cases.title: Writing Testable Code
use_cases.description: Understand how to structure services for testability, use constructor injection, and write effective unit and integration tests with proper mocking.
use_cases.target_user: Developers improving test coverage in existing applications

prompt_templates.title: Project Structure Question
prompt_templates.prompt: How should I organize a new Spring Boot project? What package structure should I use for a large application?
prompt_templates.scenario: When starting a new project and need architectural guidance

prompt_templates.title: Dependency Injection Help
prompt_templates.prompt: What is the recommended way to inject dependencies in Spring Boot? Should I use constructor injection or field injection?
prompt_templates.scenario: When setting up service classes and need DI guidance

prompt_templates.title: REST API Development
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a RESTful API for a user management feature. What should my controller look like?
prompt_templates.scenario: When building new API endpoints and need design guidance

prompt_templates.title: Configuration Best Practices
prompt_templates.prompt: How should I manage different configurations for dev, staging, and production environments in Spring Boot?

output_examples.input: How should I structure my Spring Boot project?
output_examples.output: Use a package-by-feature structure instead of package-by-layer. Organize code as com.example.app.order, com.example.app.user rather than com.example.app.controller, com.example.app.service. Use Spring Boot starters for dependencies and prefer Maven or Gradle for build management.

output_examples.input: What is the best way to handle errors in my REST API?
output_examples.output: Use @ControllerAdvice to create a global exception handler. This centralizes error handling logic and provides consistent error responses. Return proper HTTP status codes and include error details like message, timestamp, and path in your response.

best_practices: Use constructor-based dependency injection with private final fields for immutability and easier testing
Externalize configuration using application.yml and @ConfigurationProperties for type-safe property binding
Use Spring Data JPA with parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection vulnerabilities
anti_patterns: Exposing JPA entities directly to clients via REST endpoints instead of using DTOs
Using field injection (@Autowired on fields) instead of constructor injection
Hardcoding secrets in source code instead of using environment variables or secret management tools

faq.question: Should I use Maven or Gradle for my Spring Boot project?
faq.answer: Both Maven and Gradle are supported by Spring Boot. Gradle offers more concise build scripts and faster builds, while Maven has a larger ecosystem and simpler IDE integration. Choose based on team familiarity.

faq.question: What is the recommended way to structure packages in a Spring Boot application?
faq.answer: Organize packages by feature or domain rather than by technical layer. For example, use com.example.app.user and com.example.app.order instead of com.example.app.controller and com.example.app.service.

faq.question: How do I handle secrets in Spring Boot?
faq.answer: Never hardcode secrets. Use environment variables, Spring Cloud Config, HashiCorp Vault, or AWS Secrets Manager for managing sensitive configuration like database passwords and API keys.

faq.question: Should I use field injection or constructor injection?
faq.answer: Always prefer constructor injection. It makes dependencies explicit, enables immutability, and makes unit testing easier by allowing simple mock injection through constructors.

faq.question: How should I validate user input in REST endpoints?
faq.answer: Use Java Bean Validation (JSR 380) annotations like @Valid, @NotNull, and @Size on your DTOs. Spring will automatically validate request payloads before reaching your controller methods.

faq.question: What is the best approach for error handling in Spring Boot REST APIs?
faq.answer: Implement a global exception handler using @ControllerAdvice and @ExceptionHandler. This centralizes error handling and provides consistent error responses across all endpoints.
